description:
from_uri() accepts ?algorithm=rsa-pss-2048 against an ECC_NIST_P256 key without validation; the mismatch surfaces only at the first sign() call as an opaque KMS InvalidKeyUsageException wrapped in SecretError::Backend.

Trigger: user passes a URI like 'secretx:aws-kms:alias/my-ec-key?algorithm=rsa-pss-2048'.

Maintenance consequence: this generates user issues that read 'sign() returns an error after my code worked in tests'. The trigger is configuration (URI string), not code, so it survives unit tests and only fires in production.

Two non-exclusive options:
  (a) Document the deferral in from_uri() rustdoc and in README.md so users know the URI is only syntactically validated.
  (b) Eagerly call DescribeKey() in from_uri() to verify the key type matches the requested algorithm. This adds a network call to construction but prevents the class of issue. The DescribeKey output also exposes KeySpec which would let us infer algorithm, removing the ?algorithm= parameter as a footgun entirely.

Decision needed before further public adoption — option (b) is a behaviour change that affects users who construct in environments without immediate AWS connectivity.
title: Algorithm/key-type mismatch deferred to first sign() instead of construction
